Each turn you receive: visual frames (recent 24s window) + text memory (compressed summaries + recent thinks + pending questions). Output exactly ONE action:

1) <think>obs</think><action>silent</action>
   Use when: no question to answer, or pending question whose answer is not yet visible.

2) <think>obs</think><action>response</action><response>answer</response>
   Use when: the answer is currently visible — in frames, recent thinks, compressed summaries, or a recall_result just returned by the system.

3) <think>obs</think><action>recall</action><query>{"query":"keywords","time_range":"start-end"}</query>
   Use when: the answer is NOT in any visible source but you believe it was observed earlier. The system will search past observations and return a <recall_result> in your next input. Write 3-5 discriminative keywords (entity names + attributes), no answer values.

4) <think>obs</think><action>compress</action><summary>{"time_range":[s,e],"text":"..."}</summary>
   Use when: system sends <compress_trigger>. Summarize the specified thinks. Retain ALL entity names, visual attributes, OCR, and state changes. If no range is specified, select the oldest thinks to compress.

Think rules:
- 40-60 tokens, describe ONLY what is newly visible in the current chunk.
- No meta-reasoning, no sound/smell/emotion, no speculation.
- Maintain consistent entity names from memory (e.g., chef_1, pot_1).

queries.question: Describe the sequence of visual changes and object arrangements shown from the intro to the bartender scene.

answers.text: The provided evidence describes a completely static scene from 0 to 10 seconds, showing a layered cocktail and school text without any changes. There is no visual information regarding a bartender appearing, a bar counter, or any equipment arrangements. Consequently, the sequence of events described in the correct answer involving a man in a red shirt and gesturing cannot be confirmed or described based solely on the available evidence provided.

From 14 seconds, the scene shifts to a man in a red shirt behind a bar, gesturing with his right hand near a martini glass. By 28 seconds, he stands still with hands resting on the counter. At 30 seconds, his left hand lifts slightly near a metal shaker. Finally, between 32 and 34 seconds, he places his left hand atop the shaker while keeping his right hand flat on the counter, facing forward as if speaking.

From 34 to 36 seconds, the bartender shifts his grip, holding the clear mixing glass with his left hand and the metal shaker with his right, lifting both slightly off the counter. Immediately after, between 36 and 38 seconds, he lowers these items back onto the surface while maintaining his hold. Throughout these actions, the yellow text overlay and background liquor bottles remain static.

From 38 to 44 seconds, the bartender transitions from resting his hands to firmly gripping the clear mixing glass with his left hand and the metal shaker tin with his right. He directs his gaze downward toward these tools, preparing to mix. Throughout this sequence, the background shelves filled with liquor bottles and the yellow website text overlay at the bottom of the screen remain completely static, providing a consistent backdrop to his focused preparation.

From 44 to 52 seconds, the bartender pours liquid from a white bottle into a metal jigger held over a silver shaker tin. He maintains a downward gaze focused on the pouring action while the clear mixing glass rests on the counter to his left. After finishing the pour, he sets the bottle down and slightly turns his head right, keeping his eyes lowered as the yellow text overlay and background liquor bottles remain unchanged.

From 56 to 64 seconds, the bartender pours from an orange and white carton into the shaker, then briefly moves right before returning to center. He subsequently stands facing forward, holding a silver jigger in his right hand while gesturing with his left near the mixing glass. The metal shaker remains on the counter, and the yellow text overlay and liquor bottle background stay fixed throughout these final actions.

Each turn you receive: visual frames (recent 24s window) + text memory (compressed summaries + recent thinks + pending questions). Output exactly ONE action:

1) <think>obs</think><action>silent</action>
   Use when: no question to answer, or pending question whose answer is not yet visible.

2) <think>obs</think><action>response</action><response>answer</response>
   Use when: the answer is currently visible — in frames, recent thinks, compressed summaries, or a recall_result just returned by the system.

3) <think>obs</think><action>recall</action><query>{"query":"keywords","time_range":"start-end"}</query>
   Use when: the answer is NOT in any visible source but you believe it was observed earlier. The system will search past observations and return a <recall_result> in your next input. Write 3-5 discriminative keywords (entity names + attributes), no answer values.

4) <think>obs</think><action>compress</action><summary>{"time_range":[s,e],"text":"..."}</summary>
   Use when: system sends <compress_trigger>. Summarize the specified thinks. Retain ALL entity names, visual attributes, OCR, and state changes. If no range is specified, select the oldest thinks to compress.

Think rules:
- 40-60 tokens, describe ONLY what is newly visible in the current chunk.
- No meta-reasoning, no sound/smell/emotion, no speculation.
- Maintain consistent entity names from memory (e.g., chef_1, pot_1).

samples.output: <think>A static shot displays a tall glass filled with a layered orange and red cocktail topped with ice and an orange slice garnish on the rim. To the right, red text reads "American Bartenders School" above a red underline and the website URL "www.barschool.com" against a black background. The scene remains unchanged throughout the duration.</think><answer></answer>

samples.output: <think>A static title card appears against a black background featuring a tall glass on the left filled with a layered red and orange drink, ice, and an orange slice garnish. To the right, bold red text displays "American Bartenders School" above a red underline and the website address "www.barschool.com". The scene remains completely motionless.</think><answer></answer>

   Use when: no question to answer, or pending question whose answer is not yet visible.

   Use when: the answer is currently visible — in frames, recent thinks, compressed summaries, or a recall_result just returned by the system.

3) <think>obs</think><action>recall</action><query>{"query":"keywords","time_range":"start-end"}</query>
   Use when: the answer is NOT in any visible source but you believe it was observed earlier. The system will search past observations and return a <recall_result> in your next input. Write 3-5 discriminative keywords (entity names + attributes), no answer values.

4) <think>obs</think><action>compress</action><summary>{"time_range":[s,e],"text":"..."}</summary>
   Use when: system sends <compress_trigger>. Summarize the specified thinks. Retain ALL entity names, visual attributes, OCR, and state changes. If no range is specified, select the oldest thinks to compress.

Think rules:
- 40-60 tokens, describe ONLY what is newly visible in the current chunk.
- No meta-reasoning, no sound/smell/emotion, no speculation.
- Maintain consistent entity names from memory (e.g., chef_1, pot_1).

Each turn you receive: visual frames (recent 24s window) + text memory (compressed summaries + recent thinks + pending questions). Output exactly ONE action:

1) <think>obs</think><action>silent</action>
   Use when: no question to answer, or pending question whose answer is not yet visible.

2) <think>obs</think><action>response</action><response>answer</response>
   Use when: the answer is currently visible — in frames, recent thinks, compressed summaries, or a recall_result just returned by the system.

3) <think>obs</think><action>recall</action><query>{"query":"keywords","time_range":"start-end"}</query>
   Use when: the answer is NOT in any visible source but you believe it was observed earlier. The system will search past observations and return a <recall_result> in your next input. Write 3-5 discriminative keywords (entity names + attributes), no answer values.

4) <think>obs</think><action>compress</action><summary>{"time_range":[s,e],"text":"..."}</summary>
   Use when: system sends <compress_trigger>. Summarize the specified thinks. Retain ALL entity names, visual attributes, OCR, and state changes. If no range is specified, select the oldest thinks to compress.

Think rules:
- 40-60 tokens, describe ONLY what is newly visible in the current chunk.
- No meta-reasoning, no sound/smell/emotion, no speculation.
- Maintain consistent entity names from memory (e.g., chef_1, pot_1).

compressed_segments.text: [0-10] A static title card displays a tall clear glass filled with a layered orange and red cocktail topped with ice and an orange slice garnish on the rim. To the right, bright red text reads "American Bartenders School" above a red underline and the website URL "www.barschool.com" against a black background. The scene remains completely unchanged. [10-14] The visual cuts to a man with short brown hair wearing a red shirt standing behind a bar counter. Shelves filled with various liquor bottles line the background. On the bar surface in front of him are a mixing glass, a metal shaker tin, a jigger, and a small cup containing white liquid. He faces forward, moving his mouth slightly while standing still.

[14-18] A man in a red shirt stands behind a bar counter, gesturing with his right hand near a martini glass on the left while speaking. [18-24] He raises his right hand higher to chest level, keeping fingers spread and palm facing inward. A metal shaker, mixing glass, and jigger remain stationary on the counter, with liquor bottles on shelves behind him. [24-26] Yellow text "barschool.com" appears overlaid at the bottom center as he continues gesturing. [26-28] The man lowers his right hand, resting both palms on the counter surface near the mixing glass and shaker, while the text overlay remains visible.

The man in the red shirt stands behind a bar lined with liquor bottles, with "barschool.com" in yellow text at the bottom. From [30-34], his right hand rests near the martini and clear mixing glasses while his left hand hovers near the metal shaker tin, his mouth open as if speaking. At [34-36], he shifts his grip to hold the clear mixing glass and metal shaker tin, lifting both slightly off the counter. He lowers them back down at [36-38], maintaining his hold. From [38-42], he leans his upper body forward, directing his gaze downward toward the bar surface while his hands remain positioned near the glass and tin.

[42-44] A man in a red shirt firmly holds a clear mixing glass with his left hand and a metal shaker tin with his right, gazing downward at the tools while liquor bottles fill the shelves behind him and yellow text overlays the bottom. [44-52] He pours liquid from a white bottle into a metal jigger positioned over the silver shaker tin, finishes pouring, and sets the bottle down while the clear mixing glass rests on the counter. [52-56] He then holds a carton near the tin, tilts it with both hands, and pours a white liquid into the metal shaker tin as the 'barschool.com' text remains visible.

[56-60] A man in a red shirt pours from an orange and white carton into a metal shaker tin, looking down with yellow text 'barschool.com' at the bottom. He turns right and moves away blurred. [60-64] He returns to the center, standing facing forward with mouth open, holding a silver jigger in his right hand near a clear mixing glass. [64-68] He pours liquid from a clear bottle into the jigger held over the shaker, then sets the bottle aside and empties the jigger into the tin. Rows of liquor bottles line the shelves behind him throughout.

[68-70] The man in the red shirt looks up from the shaker tin to face forward with his mouth open. He holds the silver jigger in his right hand near the counter surface while the yellow text overlay remains fixed at the bottom center against the liquor bottles.

queries.question: What entities are present and what are they doing?

answers.text: A man in a red short-sleeved shirt stands behind a dark grey bar counter, surrounded by shelves of liquor bottles. Various bartending tools, including a cocktail shaker, mixing glass, martini glass, and jigger, are arranged on the surface. Initially, he gestures with his right hand near his chest while facing forward. Later, he places his hands on the counter, lifts the mixing glass and shaker tin slightly, and then sets them back down while appearing to speak.

He rests his right hand holding the silver jigger on the counter while his left hand sits near the clear mixing glass. The yellow text overlay remains fixed at the bottom center against the background of liquor bottles.</think><answer></answer>

input.system:
You are a streaming video agent. You observe 2-second video chunks and maintain memory.

Each turn you receive: visual frames (recent 24s window) + text memory (compressed summaries + recent thinks + pending questions). Output exactly ONE action:

1) <think>obs</think><action>silent</action>
   Use when: no question to answer, or pending question whose answer is not yet visible.

2) <think>obs</think><action>response</action><response>answer</response>
   Use when: the answer is currently visible — in frames, recent thinks, compressed summaries, or a recall_result just returned by the system.

3) <think>obs</think><action>recall</action><query>{"query":"keywords","time_range":"start-end"}</query>
   Use when: the answer is NOT in any visible source but you believe it was observed earlier. The system will search past observations and return a <recall_result> in your next input. Write 3-5 discriminative keywords (entity names + attributes), no answer values.

4) <think>obs</think><action>compress</action><summary>{"time_range":[s,e],"text":"..."}</summary>
   Use when: system sends <compress_trigger>. Summarize the specified thinks. Retain ALL entity names, visual attributes, OCR, and state changes. If no range is specified, select the oldest thinks to compress.

Think rules:
- 40-60 tokens, describe ONLY what is newly visible in the current chunk.
- No meta-reasoning, no sound/smell/emotion, no speculation.
- Maintain consistent entity names from memory (e.g., chef_1, pot_1).
